hypothesis: Research domain expansion should be driven by gradient anomaly detection — when a gradient fires in an unexpected direction (e.g., system-health gradient with high magnitude during a coding task), it indicates an untapped research domain. By systematically mapping these anomalies to research topics, we can discover high-value exploration areas that current priorities miss.
research_question: Can gradient anomaly patterns predict high-value research domains that are not covered by existing research priorities?
expected_outcome: Establish a gradient-anomaly-to-research-domain mapping protocol that identifies 3-5 high-value research areas per saturation cycle
